briefdescription.para: Get the DP AUX register data.
Set the DP AUX register data.
These constants are used to identify callback functions.
No Rx Attached.
Transmit Low value content.
Known HDCP2 Capable Rx.
Exchange Km.
substate of A1: wait for AKE_SEND_CERT.
No stored Km substate of A1: wait for AKE_SEND_H_PRIME.
No stored Km substate of A1, wait for AKE_SEND_PAIRING_INFO.
Stored substate of A1, wait for AKE_SEND_PAIRING_INFO.
Locality Check.
Locality Check.

detaileddescription.para: Receive and verify L_Prime.

briefdescription.para: Exchange Ks.
Test for repeater.
Authenticated.
Wait for receiver ID list, verify and send acknowledgment.
Wait for receiver ID list.
Verify Receiver ID List.
Send Receiver ID List acknowledgment.
Content Stream Management.
Content Stream Management.

detaileddescription.para: Receive and verify M_Prime.

briefdescription.para: Number of states in the state machine.
These constants specify the different states in the internal state machine.
A HDCP2 compatible receiver is not found.
Authentication is busy.
Authentication is completed successfully.
Authentication failed.
ReAuthentication requested.
A device in the HDCP chain is revoked.
No valid SRM is loaded.
These constants specify return values on polling with XHdcp22Tx_Dp_Poll.

detaileddescription.para: Depending on this return value, encryption of the HDMI signal should be set to encrypted or un-encrypted. See also XHdcp22Tx_Dp_EnableEncryption and XHdcp22Tx_Dp_DisableEncryption

briefdescription.para: Type 0 Content Stream.

detaileddescription.para: Stream may be transmitted to all HDCP devices.

briefdescription.para: Type 1 Content Stream.

detaileddescription.para: Stream must not be transmitted to HDCP1.x devices and HDCP2.0 Repeaters.

briefdescription.para: These constants are used to define the content stream type.
Log Event None.
State of the state machine.
Authentication result of polling.
HDCP2.2 core is enabled or disabled.
HDCP2.2 core is reset.
HDCP2.2 stream is encrypted or not.
An error was detected in one of the test modes.
Log event for debugging.
Number of times Locality check has been done.
Number of times Content Stream Management check has been done.
User logging.
Last value the list, only used for checking.
These constants are events as stored in the logging list.
Display Port protocol.
HDMI protocol.
These constants are used to define the used protocol.
Module acts as a HDCP 2.2 transmitter.
Module acts as a HDCP 2.2 repeater.
Module acts as a HDCP 2.2 converter.
These constants are used to define the used mode.
These constants are used to identify fields inside the topology structure.
Testmode is disabled.
Actual HDCP2.2 RX component is connected.
HDCP2.2 RX software component is not available and will be emulated.
HDCP2.2 RX is emulated, XHdcp22Tx_Dp_LogDisplay shows source code.
Use test keys as defined in Errata to HDCP on HDMI Specification Revision 2.2, February 09, 2015.
Last value the list, only used for checking.
HDCP 2.2 transmitter test mode enumeration.

detaileddescription.para: This enumeration defines the various test modes available for the HDCP 2.2 DisplayPort transmitter. These modes are used for testing and debugging purposes during development and validation.

simplesect.para: When using XHDCP22_TX_TESTMODE_USE_TESTKEYS, the keys used are from the HDCP specification errata and should only be used for testing, never in production.

briefdescription.para: Value definitions for debugging.

detaileddescription.para: These values are used as parameter for the XHDCP22_TX_LOG_EVT_DBG logging event.

briefdescription.para: Pointer to the state handling functions.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: The next state that should be executed.

briefdescription.para: Pointer to the transition functions going from one state to the next.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

briefdescription.para: Callback type for status.

detaileddescription.para: Callback type used for pointer to single input function.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* XHdcp22_Tx_DdcHandler)(u8 DeviceAddress, u16 ByteCount, u8 *BufferPtr, u8 Stop, void *RefPtr)
memberdef.argsstring: )(u8 DeviceAddress, u16 ByteCount, u8 *BufferPtr, u8 Stop, void *RefPtr)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22_Tx_DdcHandler

briefdescription.para: Callback type used for calling DDC read and write functions.

parameterdescription.para: is the (i2c) device address of the HDCP port.
is the amount of data bytes in the buffer to read or write.
is a pointer to a buffer that is used for reading or writing.
is a flag to control if a stop token is set or not.
is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the DDC reading and writing functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS The read action was successful.
XST_FAILURE The read action failed.

memberdef.definition: typedef u32(* Xhdcp22_Tx_Dp_RdWrHandler)(void *HandlerRef, u32 offset, u8 *buf, u32 size)
memberdef.name: Xhdcp22_Tx_Dp_RdWrHandler

briefdescription.para: Type for pointer to four input function with a return value.

memberdef.name: XHdcp22_Tx_StateTable
memberdef.initializer: = { XHdcp22Tx_StateH0, XHdcp22Tx_StateH1, XHdcp22Tx_StateA0, XHdcp22Tx_StateA1, Xhdcp22Tx_StateA1_1, Xhdcp22Tx_StateA1_Nsk0, Xhdcp22Tx_StateA1_Nsk1, Xhdcp22Tx_StateA1_Sk0, XHdcp22Tx_StateA2, XHdcp22Tx_StateA2_1, XHdcp22Tx_StateA3, XHdcp22Tx_StateA4, XHdcp22Tx_StateA5, XHdcp22Tx_StateA6_A7_A8, XHdcp22Tx_StateA6, XHdcp22Tx_StateA7, XHdcp22Tx_StateA8, XHdcp22Tx_StateA9, XHdcp22Tx_StateA9_1 }

briefdescription.para: This table contains the function pointers for all possible states.

detaileddescription.para: The order of elements must match the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp_StateType enumerator definitions.

briefdescription.para: This table is a matrix that contains the function pointers for functions to execute on state transition.

detaileddescription.para: It is filled dynamically. A NULL value is used if no transition is required.

briefdescription.para: This array contains the capabilities of the HDCP22 TX core, and is transmitted during authentication as part of the AKE_Init message.
This function is a called to initialize the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if authenticated is started successfully.
XST_FAIL if the state machine is disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function is a called to initialize the hardware random generator.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if authenticated is started successfully.
XST_FAIL if the state machine is disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function computes the subcore absolute address on axi-lite interface Subsystem is mapped at an absolute address and all included sub-cores are at pre-defined offset from the subsystem base address.

detaileddescription.para: To access the subcore register map from host CPU an absolute address is required.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the subsystem instance
is the offset of the the subcore instance
is the computed base address of the subcore instance

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if base address computation is successful and within subsystem address range else XST_FAILURE

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 DeviceAddress, u16 ByteCount, u8 *BufferPtr, u8 Stop, void *RefPtr)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_StubDdc

briefdescription.para: This function is a dummy called as long as there is no valid ddc handler set.

detaileddescription.para: It always returns an error.

parameterdescription.para: is the DDC (i2c) device address.
is the number of bytes in the buffer.
is the buffer that stores the data to be written or read.
is a flag to signal i2c stop condition.
is the reference pointer for the handler.

briefdescription.para: This function is a dummy called as long as there is no valid authenticated handler set.

detaileddescription.para: It always returns an error.

parameterdescription.para: is the reference pointer for the handler.

briefdescription.para: This function is the implementation of the HDCP transmit H0 state.

detaileddescription.para: According protocol this is the reset state that is entered initially. As soon as hot plug is detected and Rx is available, a transition to state h1 is performed. Since hotplug detect is controlled by the user, the next state is always state H1.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the implementation of the HDCP transmit H1 state.

detaileddescription.para: As soon as hot plug is detected and Rx is available, a transition to this state is performed. Hotplug detection if controlled by the user of this core. This means that this state is practically the entry state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHDCP22 TX core instance.

listitem.para: XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A0 if the attached receiver is HDCP2 capable.
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_H1 if the attached receiver is NOT HDCP2 capable.

briefdescription.para: This function is the implementation of the HDCP transmit A0 state.

detaileddescription.para: If content protection not desired, return to H1 state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHDCP22 TX core instance.

listitem.para: XHDCP22_TX_STATE_H1 if state machine is set to disabled (content protection NOT desired).
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A1 if authentication must start.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the first part of the A1 state of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes a part of the A1 state functionality of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

detaileddescription.para: It receives the certificate and decides if it should continue to a 'no stored km' or 'stored Km'intermediate state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A0 on errors.
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A1_1 if busy waiting for the XHDCP22_TX_AKE_SEND_CERT message.
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A1_SK0 for a 'Stored Km' scenario.
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A1_NSK0 for a 'Non Stored Km' scenario.

briefdescription.para: This function executes a part of the A1 state functionality of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

detaileddescription.para: It handles a part of the 'No Stored Km' scenario and receives and verifies H'

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A0 on errors.
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A1_NSK0 if busy waiting for the XHDCP22_TX_AKE_SEND_H_PRIME message.
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A1_NSK1 for the next part of the 'Stored Km' scenario.

briefdescription.para: This function executes a part of the A1 state functionality of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

detaileddescription.para: It handles a part of the 'No Stored Km' scenario and receives and stores pairing info.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A0 on errors.
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A1_NSK1 if busy waiting for the XHDCP22_TX_AKE_SEND_PAIRING_INFO message.
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A2 if authentication is done.

briefdescription.para: This function executes a part of the A1 state functionality of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

detaileddescription.para: It handles a part of the 'Stored Km' scenario and receives and verifies H'.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A0 on errors.
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A1_SK0 if busy waiting for the
XHDCP22_TX_AKE_SEND_H_PRIME message.
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A2 if authentication is done.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the A2 state of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes a part of the A2 state functionality of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

detaileddescription.para: It receives and verifies L' (locality check).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A2_1 if busy waiting for the XHDCP22_TX_AKE_SEND_L_PRIME message.
XHDCP22_TX_STATE_A3 if authentication is done.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the A3 state of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the A4 state of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the A5 state of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

detaileddescription.para: Before authentication is granted, a 200 ms mandatory wait is added, so the integrating application does not start the cipher premature.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the states A6, A7 and A8 of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

detaileddescription.para: Within these states the HDCP2.2 TX receives and verifies the receiver ID list. The HDCP2.2 TX is required to send a response within 2 seconds after the HDCP2.2 repeater has made the receiver ID list available. These states have been grouped in order to guarantee that the HDCP2.2 TX responds as fast as it can.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the A6 state of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the A7 state of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the A8 state of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the first portion of the A9 state of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the second portion of the A9 state of the HDCP2.2 TX protocol.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes on transition from state A1 or one of its sub states to A0, which means that an error in authentication has occurred.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes on transition from state A1_SK0 or A1_NSK1 to A2, which means that state A2 is executed for the first time.

detaileddescription.para: The locality check counter is initialized. Subsequent locality checks are then executed for an extra 1023 times.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes on transition from state A2 or one of its substates to A0, which means that an error in authentication has occurred.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes on transition from state A3 to A0, which means that an error in authentication has occurred.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes on transition from state A3 to A4, which means that the Session Key Exchange (SKE) has completed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes on transition from state A4 to A5, which means that an authentication has succeeded and state A5 is executed the first time.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes on transition from state A6 or A7 to A0, which means that an error in authentication has occurred.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function executes on transition from state A9 to A0, which means that an error in the content stream management has occurred.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function issues a AKE_Init message.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
the buffer to use for messaging.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if writing succeeded
XST_FAILURE if failed to write.

briefdescription.para: This function issues a AKE_No_Stored_km message.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
the buffer containing the certificate and re-used for sending the message.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if writing succeeded
XST_FAILURE if failed to write

briefdescription.para: This function issues a AKE_Stored_km message.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
the buffer to use for messaging.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if writing succeeded
XST_FAILURE if failed to write

briefdescription.para: This function write a locality check has value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if writing succeeded
XST_FAILURE if failed to write

briefdescription.para: This function sends the session key to the receiver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
is a pointer to the encrypted session key.
is a pointer to the random IV pair.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if writing succeeded
XST_FAILURE if failed to write

briefdescription.para: This function sends the Type Value to the receiver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if writing succeeded
XST_FAILURE if failed to write

briefdescription.para: This function sends the Receiver ID List acknowledgement to the repeater.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
is a pointer to the least significant 128-bits of V.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if writing succeeded
XST_FAILURE if failed to write

briefdescription.para: This function sends the Content Stream Type to the repeater.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if writing succeeded
XST_FAILURE if failed to write

briefdescription.para: This function receives a message send by HDCP22 RX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
the message Id of the expected message.
the size of the expected message.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the message size and id are as expected.
XST_FAILURE if if the message size and id are incorrect.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pseudo random 64-bit value for Rtx as part of the HDCP22 TX initiation message.

detaileddescription.para:
If appropriate it is overwritten with a test vector.
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
RtxPtr
is a 64-bit random value;
None.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pseudo random 128-bit value for Km as part of the HDCP22 TX initiation message.

detaileddescription.para: If appropriate it is overwritten with a test vector.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
is a pointer to a 128 bits random value.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pseudo random 64-bit value for Rn as part of the locality check.

detaileddescription.para: If appropriate it is overwritten with a test vector.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
is a pointer to a 256 bits random seed value.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pseudo random 64-bit value for Rn as part of the HDCP22 TX locality check.

detaileddescription.para: If appropriate it is overwritten with a test vector.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
is a pointer to a 64 bits random value.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pseudo random 64-bit value for Riv as part of the HDCP22 TX session key exchange message.

detaileddescription.para:
If appropriate it is overwritten with a test vector.
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
RivPtr
is a 64-bit random value;
None.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pseudo random 128-bit value for Ks as part of the HDCP22 TX session key exchange message.

detaileddescription.para:
If appropriate it is overwritten with a test vector.
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
KsPtr
is a 128-bit random value;
None.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the DCP LLC public key If appropriate it returns a test vector.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
is a 64-bit random value;

briefdescription.para: This function gets a stored pairing info entry from the storage.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to a 5-byte receiver Id.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the found ReceiverId or NULL if the pairing info wasn't stored yet.

briefdescription.para: This function invalidates a pairing info structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to a 5-byte receiver Id.

briefdescription.para: This function updates a pairing info entry in the storage.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to a pairing info structure.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if there was enough room
XST_FAILURE if there was no room for this receiver

briefdescription.para: This function starts the timer needed for checking the HDCP22 Receive status register.

detaileddescription.para: In case the timer is started to receive a message, the MsgAvailable flag is reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
is the timeout for the next status update.

briefdescription.para: This function waits for receiving expected messages from the receiver.

detaileddescription.para: If the timer is not started, it will be started. This has to be used to avoid blocking waits, and allows polling to return to allow the main thread to continue handling other requests. Some receivers require to read status as soon as possible otherwise the receiver may request for a re-authentication so we must poll!

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
indicates the expected message size in bytes.

indicates if the repeater ready bit from RxStatus register should be used or the specified ExpectedSize.
TRUE Ready bit from RxStatus is used. ExpectedSize will be ignored.
FALSE ExpectedSize is used. Ready bit from RxStatus will be ignored.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if no problems
XST_FAILURE if the receiver has timed out

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current timer count value of the expiration timer.

detaileddescription.para: register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
is the timeout for the next status update.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the timer count could be acquired.
XST_FAILURE if not.

briefdescription.para: This function reads RxStatus from the DDC channel.

detaileddescription.para: If the read is not successful it will default to an RxStatus value of 0xFFFF to initiate re-authentication.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
the buffer to use for messaging.

briefdescription.para: This function handles authentication failures.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function handles reauthentication requests.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the DEPTH in the repeater topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the DEVICE_COUNT in the repeater topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: DEVICE_COUNT in the repeater topology structure.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the MAX_DEVICS_EXCEEDED flag in the repeater topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: MAX_DEVICS_EXCEEDED flag in the repeater structure.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the MAX_CASCADE_EXCEEDED flag in the repeater topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: MAX_CASCADE_EXCEEDED flag in the repeater structure.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the HDCP2_LEGACY_DEVICE_DOWNSTREAM flag in the repeater topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: HDCP2_LEGACY_DEVICE_DOWNSTREAM flag in the repeater structure.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the HDCP1_DEVICE_DOWNSTREAM flag in the repeater topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: HDCP1_DEVICE_DOWNSTREAM flag in the repeater structure.

briefdescription.para: This function Writes a complete message into the DPCD registers of remote device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: For DP there are no msg_ids for all the HDCP msgs, But to make less changes in the already existed HDCP22 statmachine which is written for HDMI, Assigned msg_ids for each msg.

briefdescription.para: This function reads requested MSG from DP RX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
the message Id of the expected message.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the message size and id are as expected.
XST_FAILURE if if the message size and id are incorrect.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the HDCP22 TX core.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the HDCP TX core. Initialization of the HDCP TX includes setting up the instance data and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
points to the configuration structure associated with the HDCP TX core.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS Initialization was successful.
XST_FAILURE Initialization of the internal timer failed or there was a HDCP TX PIO ID mismatch.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to load the Lc128 value by copying the contents of the array referenced by Lc128Ptr into the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
is a pointer to an array.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to load the system renewability messages (SRMs) which carries the Receiver ID revocation list.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
is a pointer to an array.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if loaded SRM successfully.
XST_FAILURE if SRM signature verification failed.

briefdescription.para: This function searches for the specified ReceiverID in the revocation list.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
is a pointer to the ReceiverID to lookup

listitem.para: TRUE if Receiver ID was found in the revocation list
FALSE if Receiver ID was not found in the revocation list

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pointer to the Revocation Receiver ID list.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pointer to the downstream topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp_Topology structure or NULL when the topology info is invalid.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a pointer to the ReceiverID list in the repeater topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the ReceiverID list in the topology structure.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get various fields inside the topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
indicates what field of the topology structure to get.

simplesect.para: Value associated to field inside the topology structure.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Content Stream Type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp instance.
specifies the content stream type.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the version.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: Returns the version register of the cipher.

briefdescription.para: This function is a called to start authentication.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if authenticated is started successfully.
XST_FAIL if the state machine is disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function is a executed every time to trigger the state machine.

detaileddescription.para: The user of HDCP22 TX is responsible to call this function on a regular base.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: Enumerated authentication status defined in XHdcp22_Tx_Dp_AuthenticationType.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the state machine.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the state machine and acts as a resume.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the state machine and acts as a pause.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables HDMI stream encryption by enabling the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if authenticated is started successfully.
XST_FAIL if the state machine is disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function disables HDMI stream encryption by disabling the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the blank output for the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the blank output for the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current repeater mode status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the HDCP 2.2 instance is part of the downstream port of a repeater
FALSE if the HDCP 2.2 instance is a transmitter

briefdescription.para: This function sets the repeater mode status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp instance.
is TRUE to enable the repeater mode and FALSE to disable.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current enabled state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current encryption enabled state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: TRUE if encryption is enabled otherwise FALSE.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current progress state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: TRUE if authentication is in progress otherwise FALSE.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current authenticated state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the downstream device HDCP2Version register is set.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para:
- TRUE if downstream device is HDCP 2.2 capable.
FALSE if downstream device is not HDCP 2.2 capable.

briefdescription.para: This function installs callback functions for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ------------------------- --------------------------- (XHDCP22_TX_HANDLER_DDC_WRITE) DdcWrite (XHDCP22_TX_HANDLER_DDC_READ) DdcRead (XHDCP22_TX_HANDLER_AUTHENTICATED) AuthenticatedCallback (XHDCP22_TX_HANDLER_UNAUTHENTICATED) UnauthenticatedCallback (XHDCP22_TX_HANDLER_DOWNSTREAM_TOPOLOGY_AVAILABLE) DownstreamTopologyAvailableCallback

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX core instance.
specifies the type of handler.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.
XST_INVALID_PARAM when HandlerType is invalid.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to the internal timer control instance needed for connecting the timer interrupt to an interrupt controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the internal timer control instance.

briefdescription.para: This function handles timer interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: refers to a XHdcp22_Tx_Dp structure
the number of used timer.

simplesect.para: For dp design, this will be called form the Tx subsystem's timer handler so it should be a public function.

briefdescription.para: This function can be used to change the polling value.

detaileddescription.para: The polling value is the amount of time in milliseconds to wait between successive reads of the RxStatus register. The RxStatus register is polled to determine when a message is available for reading during authentication or during the link integrity check phase to determine when to issue re-authentication. The polling value needs to be at most 20ms to account for the locality check.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

is the polling interval defined in milliseconds
0 : Always Poll
1 : Poll after 1ms
2 : Poll after 2ms
3 : Poll after 3ms etc...

briefdescription.para: This function clear the global pairing info structure, so every HDCP2.2 receiver will have to go through the 'no stored km' sequence to authenticate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function adds a ReceiverID to RevocationList.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22Tx core instance.
is a pointer to a 5-byte receiver Id.

simplesect.para: Supposed to be called by Upstream Content Control Function

briefdescription.para: This function clears the log pointers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
allows to add debug logging.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the time expired since a log reset was called.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

simplesect.para: The expired logging time in useconds.

briefdescription.para: This function writes HDCP TX logs into buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
specifies an action to be carried out. Please refer XHdcp22_Tx_Dp_LogEvt enum in xhdcp22_tx_dp.h.
specifies the information that gets written into log buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function provides the log information from the log buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

listitem.para: Content of log buffer if log pointers are not equal.
Otherwise Zero.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the content of log buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDCP22 TX core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the state machine information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDCP22 TX core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is to set the protocol (HDMI/DP) over which HDCP22 is happening.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
is the protocol HDMI/DP.

briefdescription.para: This function is to attach the HDCP22 timer instance to DP TX subsystem's timer.

detaileddescription.para: For the DP design the timer is being used from the DP TX subsystem. So the HDCP22 timer instance will be attached in DpTxSs initialization.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.
is the DpTx susbsystem's timer instance.

simplesect.para: For the DP design the timer is being used from the DP TX subsystem. So the HDCP22 timer instance will be attached in DpTxSs initialization.

briefdescription.para: This function is to read the RX_STATUS when a cp_irq received.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function set the lane count of an hdcp22 interface.

parameterdescription.para: is the transmitter instance.
is the number of lanes of the interface.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful.

briefdescription.para: This function set the MST mode of an hdcp22 interface.

parameterdescription.para: is the transmitter instance.
enables the MST configuration of the interface.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a reference to an XHdcp22_Tx_Dp_Config structure based on the core id, DeviceId.

detaileddescription.para: The return value will refer to an entry in the device configuration table defined in the xhdcp22_tx_g.c file.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique core ID of the HDCP2.2 TX core for the lookup operation.

simplesect.para: XHdcp22Tx_Dp_LookupConfig returns a reference to a config record in the configuration table (in xhdcp22_tx_g.c) corresponding to DeviceId, or NULL if no match is found.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Seed, unsigned int Seedlen, u8 *Mask, unsigned int MaskLen)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_Mgf1

briefdescription.para: This function is the RSA OAEP masking function.

parameterdescription.para: is the masking seed to use.
is the size of the masking seed .
is the output mask.
is size of the mask.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *KeyPubNPtr, int KeyPubNSize, const u8 *KeyPubEPtr, int KeyEPubSize, const u8 *Message, const u32 MessageLen, const u8 *MaskingSeed, u8 *EncryptedMessage)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_RsaOaepEncrypt

briefdescription.para: This function implements the RSAES-OAEP-Encrypt operation.

detaileddescription.para: The message is encoded using EME-OAEP and then encrypted with the public key using RSAEP.
Reference: PKCS#1 v2.1, Section 7.1.1

parameterdescription.para: is the public certificate modulus.
is the size public certificate modulus.
is the public certificate exponent.
is the public certificate exponent size.
is the octet string to be encrypted.
is the length of octet string to be encrypted and must be less than or equal to (nLen - 2*hLen - 2)
is the random octet string seed of length hLen used by EME-OAEP encoding function.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Message, const u32 MessageLen, const u8 *MaskingSeed, u8 *EncodedMessage)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_Pkcs1EmeOaepEncode

briefdescription.para: This function implements EME-OAEP encoding.

detaileddescription.para: The label L is the empty string and the underlying hash function is SHA256.
Reference: PKCS#1 v2.1, Section 7.1.1, Part 2

parameterdescription.para: is the octet string to be encoded
is the length of the octet string to be encoded.
is the random octet string seed of length hLen.
is the 128 byte encoded octet string.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *KeyPubNPtr, int KeyPubNSize, const u8 *KeyPubEPtr, int KeyPubESize, const u8 *MsgPtr, int MsgSize, u8 *EncryptedMsgPtr)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_RsaEncryptMsg

briefdescription.para: RSA verification primitive recovers the message representative from the signature representative under the control of a public key.

detaileddescription.para: This is the same as encryption, but it in a totally different context.

parameterdescription.para: is the RSA public key modulus part.
is size of the RSA public key modulus in bytes.
is the RSA public key E (exponential) part.
is the sizeof the RSA public key E (exponential) part in bytes.
is the data to verify (for example a cryptographic signature).
is the size of the data to verify.
is the resulting data for verification.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if encryption succeeds.
XST_FAILURE if the message buffer size != key size.

simplesect.para: Note: Verification with a public key is the same as RSA encoding.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *MessagePtr, int MessageSize, const u8 *SignaturePtr, const u8 *KpubDcpNPtr, int KpubDcpNSize, const u8 *KpubDcpEPtr, int KpubDcpESize)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_RsaSignatureVerify

briefdescription.para: This function implements RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5-Verify for signature verification.

detaileddescription.para: The encoding scheme used to generate EM is EMSA-PKCS1-v1_5. The signature size is assumed to be 3072 bits. The underlying hash function is SHA256.
Reference: PKCS#1 v2.1, Section 8.2.2 and Section 9.2

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the start of the message to be hashed.
is the size of the message to be hashed.
is a pointer to the start of the DCP generated signature.
is a pointer to the N-value of the certificate signature.
is the size of the N-value of the certificates signature.
iis a pointer to the E-value of the certificate signature.
is the size of the E-value of the certificates signature.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the certificate is valid.
XST_FAILURE if the certificate is invalid.

briefdescription.para: This function generates random octets.

detaileddescription.para:
Otherwise it uses offloading from hardware.
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx_Dp instance to be worked on.
NumOctets
is the number of octets in the random number.
RandomNumberPtr
is a pointer to the random number.
None.

memberdef.argsstring: (const XHdcp22_Tx_CertRx *CertificatePtr, const u8 *KpubDcpNPtr, int KpubDcpNSize, const u8 *KpubDcpEPtr, int KpubDcpESize)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_VerifyCertificate

briefdescription.para: This function verifies the HDCP receivers certificate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the certificate from the HDCP2.2 receiver.
is a pointer to the N-value of the certificate signature.
is the size of the N-value of the certificates signature.
iis a pointer to the E-value of the certificate signature.
is the size of the E-value of the certificates signature.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the certificate is valid.
XST_FAILURE if the certificate is invalid.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *SrmPtr, int SrmSize, const u8 *KpubDcpNPtr, int KpubDcpNSize, const u8 *KpubDcpEPtr, int KpubDcpESize)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_VerifySRM

briefdescription.para: This function verifies a HDCP2 system renewability message (SRM) block.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the SRM.
is the size of the SRM in bytes.
is a pointer to the N-value of the DCP LLC key.
is the size of the N-value of the DCP LLC key.
iis a pointer to the E-value of the DCP LLC key.
is the size of the E-value of the DCP LLC key.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the SRM block is valid.
XST_FAILURE if the SRM block is invalid.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Rrx, const u8 *RxCaps, const u8 *Rtx, const u8 *TxCaps, const u8 *Km, u8 *HPrime)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_ComputeHPrime

briefdescription.para: This function computes HPrime.

parameterdescription.para: is the Rx random generated value on start of authentication.
are the capabilities of the receiver.
is the Tx random generated value start of authentication.
are the capabilities of the receiver.
is the master key generated by tx.
is a pointer to the HPrime hash from the HDCP2.2 receiver.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Rn, const u8 *Km, const u8 *Ks, const u8 *Rrx, const u8 *Rtx, u8 *EdkeyKs)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_ComputeEdkeyKs

briefdescription.para: This function computes EdkeyKs.

detaileddescription.para: Kd is calculated as in XHdcp22Tx_ComputeHPrime, but could also be stored the first time. This could save some computing time. According protocol however, only Rrx and Rtx are supposed to be stored.

parameterdescription.para: is a pseudo-random nonce.
is the master key generated by tx.. See also XHdcp22Tx_ComputeHPrime
is the session key.
is the random value generated by rx.
is the random value generated by tx.
is the encrypted Ks output.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Rn, const u8 *Km, const u8 *Rrx, const u8 *Rtx, u8 *LPrime)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_ComputeLPrime

briefdescription.para: This function computes LPrime.

detaileddescription.para: Kd is calculated as in XHdcp22Tx_ComputeHPrime, but could also be stored the first time. This could save some computing time. According protocol however, only Rrx and Rtx are supposed to be stored.

parameterdescription.para: is a pseudo-random nonce.
is the master key generated by tx and previouslyd stored.
is the random value generated by rx.
is the random value generated by tx.
is a pointer to the computed LPrime hash.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Rn, const u8 *Rrx, const u8 *RxInfo, const u8 *Rtx, const u8 *RecvIDList, const u8 RecvIDCount, const u8 *SeqNum_V, const u8 *Km, u8 *V)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_ComputeV

briefdescription.para: This function computes the HDCP 2.2 verification value V using the provided transmitter, receiver, and session parameters.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the transmitter nonce.
Pointer to the receiver nonce.
Pointer to receiver information data.
Pointer to the transmitter random value.
Pointer to the list of receiver IDs.
Number of receiver IDs in RecvIDList.
Pointer to the sequence number used for V computation.
Pointer to the session key.
Pointer to the output buffer where computed V is stored.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Rn, const u8 *Rrx, const u8 *Rtx, const u8 *StreamIDType, const u8 *k, const u8 *SeqNum_M, const u8 *Km, u8 *M)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_ComputeM

briefdescription.para: Computes the authentication value M used in the HDCP 2.2 protocol.

detaileddescription.para: This function calculates the M value by combining various authentication parameters according to the HDCP 2.2 specification for DisplayPort transmitters.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the random number Rn.
Pointer to the receiver's random number Rrx.
Pointer to the transmitter's random number Rtx.
Pointer to the stream ID type value.
Pointer to the k parameter value.
Pointer to the sequence number for M computation.
Pointer to the master key Km.
Pointer to the output buffer where the computed authentication value M will be stored.

simplesect.para: The caller must ensure all input pointers are valid and point to properly sized buffers. The V output buffer must be pre-allocated with sufficient size to store the computed hash.

briefdescription.para: This function encrypts the Km value with the receivers public key into Ekh(Km)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the certificate from the HDCP2.2 receiver.
is the random master key.
is a pointer to a 32 byte seed for masking in the MGF1 function used in RSA-OEAP encryption.
output of the encryption with a 128 byte (1024 bit) size.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if encryption succeeded.
XST_FAILURE if encryption failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 *Output, const u8 *InputA, const u8 *InputB, unsigned int Size)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_MemXor

briefdescription.para: This function calculates a XOR on a array.

parameterdescription.para: is the XOR of the input arrays.
is a input array.
is a input array.
is the size of the buffers to XOR.

briefdescription.para: RxStatus value used to force re-authentication.
Case replacement to copy a case Id to a string with a pre-lead.
prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros
This value is the default polling interval defined in milliseconds.

detaileddescription.para: The polling interval defines the amount of time to wait between successive reads of the RxStatus register.

briefdescription.para: Needed storage to transmit/receive messages to the HDCP2.2 receiver This includes 1 extra byte for the DDC address used.
Needed storage for the Device IDs in the revocation list.
The list of maximum pairing info items to store.
The size of the log buffer.
Size RSA encryption parameters to support 384 byte maximum modulus size.
prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Maximum allowed re-checking locality, prescribed by LLC.
Maximum allowed re-checking content stream management.
Lc128 global constant size.
Unique receiver Id size in bytes.
Max number of downstream devices allowed.
Max cascade depth.
Undefined.
AKE Init message.
AKE Init message size.
AKE Send Certificate message.
AKE Send Certificate message size.
AKE No Stored Km message.
AKE No Stored Km message size.
AKE Stored Km message size.
AKE Stored Km message size.
AKE H' message.
AKE H' message size.
AKE Pairing info message.
AKE Pairing info message size.
LC Init message.
LC Init message size.
Send L' message.
Send L' message size.
Send Eks message.
Send Eks message size.
Repeater Auth send receiver ID list message.
Repeater Auth send receiver ID list maximum message size.
RepeaterAuth send ack message.
RepeaterAuth send ack message size in bytes.
RepeaterAuth stream manage message.
RepeaterAuth stream manage message size in bytes.
RepeaterAuth stream ready message.
RepeaterAuth stream ready message size in bytes.
ContenType message.
Reason why the timer was started: Undefined.
Reason why the timer was started: Waiting for Content Stream Type to be set when in repeater mode.

simplesect.para: : The message ids also double as a reason identifier. Thus, the value of this define should NOT overlap a message Id.

briefdescription.para: Reason why the timer was started: Mandatory wait of 200 ms before the cipher may be activated.

detaileddescription.para:
Authenticated flag is only set after this period has expired.
: The message ids also double as a reason identifier. Thus, the value of this define should NOT overlap a message Id.

briefdescription.para: Reason why the timer was started: Status checking.

simplesect.para: : The message ids also double as a reason identifier. Thus, the value of this define should NOT overlap a message Id.

briefdescription.para: Internal used timer counter for timeout checking.
Internal used timer counter for logging.
DDC version offset.
DDC write message buffer offset.
DDC RX status offset.
DDC read message buffer offset.
HDCP Port DDC Rx status register masks.

detaileddescription.para: RX status REAUTHENTICATION bit.

briefdescription.para: RX status READY bit.
RX status available bytes in read message buffer.
RX certificate and Tx public key sizes in bytes.

detaileddescription.para: Unique receiver Id size in the RX certificate.

briefdescription.para: Public key-N size in the RX certificate.
Public key-E size in the RX certificate.
Reserved size in the RX certificate.
Signature size in the RX certificate.
Total size of the RX certificate.
RX capabilities size.
TX capabilities size.
LLC public key-N size.
LLC public key-E size.
SHA256 hash size in bytes.
AES128 keys in bytes.
64 bits.
64 bits.
1024 bits.
256 bits.
128 bits.
64-bits.
64-bits.
256 bits.
128 bits.
128 bits.
Receiver Id size in the SRM block.
Signature size in the SRM block.
RxInfo size in bytes.
Seq_num_v size in bytes.
V size in bytes.
VPrime size in bytes.
Seq_num_m size in bytes.
K size in bytes.
Stream ID and Type size in bytes.
MPrime size in bytes.
Use a certificate test vector.
Use a H_Prime test vector.
Use a L_Prime test vector.
Use a pairing info Ekh(Km) test vector, use i.c.w XHDCP22_TX_TEST_RCV_TIMEOUT.
Invalidate a value.
Timeout on a received message.
Use a V_Prime test vector.
Use a M_Prime test vector.
AKE is forced using a stored Km scenario.

detaileddescription.para: Pairing info is pre-loaded with test vectors that forces Stored Km scenario.

briefdescription.para: Disable timeout checking.

detaileddescription.para: this is mainly used to check the HDCP RX core in loopback mode in case it cannot meet timing requirements (no offloading to hardware) and response times need to be logged.

briefdescription.para: Pairing info is cleared, to force a non-stored Km scenario.
Use testvectors for receiver R1.
DDC base address (0x74 >> 1)
This file contains the main implementation of the Xilinx HDCP 2.2 Transmitter device driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains data which is shared between files and internal to the XIntc component.
This is the file for Xilinx HDCP 2.2 transmitter cryptographic functionality.
This is the main header file for Xilinx HDCP 2.2 Transmitter device driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 jb 02/21/19 Initial release 3.00 jb 12/24/21 File name changed from xhdcp22_tx.c to xhdcp22_tx_dp.c Also all APIs and structure names are added with suffix _dp.

detaileddescription.para:
The HDCP 2.2 Transmitter driver implements the authentication state machine. It consists of:
A state machine handling the states as specified in the HDCP revision 2.2 specification.
Functionality for checking if the HDCP 2.2 Receiver sink does respond within specified times.
Message handling from/to the HDCP 2.2 receiver sink.
Logging functionality including time stamps.
The driver uses a hardware timer interrupt. The interrupt controller that should be used, must be passed with the #XHdcp22Tx_SetInterruptController function.

Application developer needs to register interrupt handler with the processor, within their examples. Whenever processor calls registered application's interrupt handler associated with interrupt id, application's interrupt handler needs to call appropriate peripheral interrupt handler reading peripheral's Status register.
This driver is not thread safe. Any needs for threads or thread mutual exclusion must be satisfied by the layer above this driver.

Asserts are used within all Xilinx drivers to enforce constraints on argument values. Asserts can be turned off on a system-wide basis by defining at compile time, the NDEBUG identifier. By default, asserts are turned on and it is recommended that users leave asserts on during development.
The HDCP TX driver is composed of several source files. This allows the user to build and link only those parts of the driver that are necessary.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 jb 02/21/19 Initial release 3.00 jb 12/24/21 File name changed from xhdcp22_tx.h to xhdcp22_tx_dp.h Also all APIs and structure names are added with suffix _dp.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 jb 02/21/19 Initial release 3.00 jb 12/24/21 File name changed from xhdcp22_tx_crypt.c to xhdcp22_tx_dp_crypt.c

detaileddescription.para: It is intended for internal use only.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 jb 02/21/19 Initial release 3.00 jb 12/24/21 File name changed from xhdcp22_tx_i.c to xhdcp22_tx_dp_i.c
